data: Epitaph of Neon son of Donax
Un esercizio di scrittura su una stele da Contrada Piombo (Camarina)

Fabio Copani, «Un esercizio di scrittura su una stele da Contrada Piombo (Camarina)», in Dinamiche culturali ed etniche nella Sicilia orientale dall’età classica all’epoca ellenistica, a c. di Teresa Alfieri Tonini e Stefano Struffolino, Aristonothos, Quaderni 4 (Trento: Tangram Edizioni Scientifiche, 2014), 197–201
Stele in the local limestone, damaged below.
The first two lines are centred and regular, but increasingly irregular thereafter.

The first two lines show neat broad regular letters of late classical/early hellenistic date: N with raised right hasta; E with shorter middle bar; omega large and open with short horizontal tails; sigma with open top and bottom strokes, alpha broad with straight bar. After line 2 the letters become more irregular
Contrada Piombo, localita Bellaccio, rescue excavation by G. Di Stefano (undated), area of a small necropolis
Comprehensive collection of archaeological material discovered at the site of Kamarina
Camarina. Le acquisizioni, il museo tematico, la collezione "Biagio Pace", il lapidario
G. Di Stefano, Camarina. Le acquisizioni, il museo tematico, la collezione «Biagio Pace», il lapidario (Ispica: Tipografia Kromatografica / Il Museo Regionale di Camarina, 2013)

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Copani 2014 with slight changes based on photograph

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Neon son of Donax

commentary

The first two lines have the clear and standard form of a simple funerary stele. The complication arises with the increasingly illegible, irregular and seemingly incoherent letters which follow from line 3, and which seem in part at least to repeat the material of lines 1-2. Copani proposes that it should perhaps be understood as some sort of 'writing exercise' by a second, less-skilled lapicide, although this seems peculiar on a funerary stele.

After line 2 the letters become more irregular
